title: monitoring — compliance evidence
description: Monitoring IS the compliance evidence for ISO 27001 A.12.4 (event logging + monitoring), SOC 2 CC7.2 (anomaly detection + incident response), and NIST 800-53 IR-4 (incident handling). This module is the record-keeping system that shows regulators the tenant has an operating incident-response process. Provider credentials encrypted at rest per SOC 2 CC6.1.

controls.evidence: Every alert fire + acknowledgment + resolution writes to audits with 7-year retention. monitoring_alerts + monitoring_incidents preserve the operational trail for 30d/90d hot/cold (Enterprise: 90d/1y; P1 indefinite). Every notification attempt is logged in the notification-attempts table.

monitoring_provider_configs.config (credentials) is encrypted at rest via the compliance/encryption service. monitoring_alerts + monitoring_incidents are append-only after terminal status (observer refuses mutations). The audit trail is immutable per the audit module's design.

Every admin action (health check CRUD, alert policy CRUD, provider CRUD, circuit-breaker reset, alert/incident acknowledge/resolve) writes to audits with created_by/updated_by/deleted_by userstamps. Support + ops access via the platform plane is separately audited.

monitoring_incidents timeline (opened → acknowledged → investigating → mitigating → resolved → postmortem) formalises the ISO 27001 A.16.1 incident-response lifecycle. Every P1 incident triggers MonitoringPostmortemRequiredNotification. MTTA + MTTR are measurable via the histograms in metrics.json.

This module IS the SOC 2 CC7.1 evidence. Health checks probe infrastructure; alerts fire on threshold breach; incidents formalise the response. The monitoring dashboard + platform ops cross-tenant view provide the auditor with the ongoing operational proof.

Health-check-driven anomaly detection + threshold-triggered alerts + severity-based fan-out to paging providers + escalation SLA + postmortem workflow = the full CC7.2 apparatus. Every fired P1 alert pages within seconds; MTTA target < 5min; MTTR target < 4h.

Provider credentials encrypted at rest. Access to view unredacted config restricted to owner + admin roles. Support role sees redacted view. Every config change writes to audits with 7-year retention.

All 6 providers use TLS 1.2+ endpoints. Guzzle enforces TLS + certificate validation. HMAC signing (custom_webhook) uses SHA256. Provider credentials never logged; provider config is encrypted at rest.

Per-tenant per-provider circuit breaker + retry with backoff + failover between paging providers = defense-in-depth against provider outages. An open circuit-breaker fires MonitoringProviderCircuitBreakerOpened (P1) which pages via the failover provider.
Auditable trait writes to audits on every monitoring row mutation. Retention: 7 years.
monitoring:audit-suppressions Artisan command produces the suppression audit report. The platform dashboard cross-tenant view enables ops review of P1 incidents across all tenants.
The monitoring module IS an audit generator (secondary to the audit module). Every alert + incident lifecycle event is preserved with tenant_id + severity + timestamps.
monitoring_incidents lifecycle (open → acknowledged → investigating → mitigating → resolved → postmortem) is the IR-4 apparatus. Escalation SLA + notification routing = IR-4(1) automated support.
The monitoring dashboard read-model + platform ops cross-tenant view provide the IR-5 ongoing monitoring surface.

MonitoringIncidentOpenedNotification + MonitoringIncidentEscalatedNotification + MonitoringPostmortemRequiredNotification = IR-6 reporting. Notifications route to tenant admin + platform ops per severity.

monitoring_alerts retain 30d/90d hot/cold (Enterprise: 90d/1y). Combined with the underlying audit module's 7-year retention, the audit trail exceeds PCI-DSS 10.7's 1-year-hot + 6-years-archive requirement.
monitoring:audit-suppressions + the platform dashboard cross-tenant view provide the daily-review surface required by PCI-DSS 10.6.

This module IS the technical measure required by Art. 32(1)(d) — regular testing, assessing, and evaluating the effectiveness of technical + organisational measures for ensuring the security of processing. Health checks + alerts + incidents formalise the security-monitoring evidence.

MonitoringIncidentOpened (P1) fires within 5 minutes of detection. Combined with the audit module's breach-notification workflow, the tenant satisfies Art. 33's 72-hour notification-to-supervisory-authority requirement.

TenantErased cascades hard-delete across all six monitoring tables via FK CASCADE. No cross-provider deletion request — monitoring providers store system-health events not user data; the tenant_id is the only correlation surface and it's erased with the tenant.
